propositions: Retired but still-set env vars and TOML keys are silently ignored or defaulted, redirecting the store or weakening security/policy without any warning.
A tombstone registry should map retired inputs to their replacement, removed-in version, and behavior (error vs warn), applied before defaults are chosen.
Error-class tombstones must raise RemovedInputError subclassing BaseException so try/except ConfigError and broad except Exception blocks cannot swallow it.
Env-var and file-presence tombstone checks must run at the top of load_config() before the cache lookup so they fire on a warm MCP cache hit.
The MCP server must fail startup or the write request hard on a retired error-class input rather than downgrade it to a per-request error.
A CI anti-regression guard compares recognised keys/env vars against a golden list and fails if a key is removed without a corresponding tombstone entry.
